title: orobanche cooperi subsp. palmeri
intro: Orobanche cooperi subsp. palmeri, also known as Palmer's broomrape, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Orobanche cooperi subsp. palmeri include its potential to aid in reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
